title: Replace global Bureau work leases with object and component scopes
goal: Keep Bureau intake and independent Registry work available by replacing long-running global repository leases with object, file, component and short effect-gate scopes.

acceptance.assertion: Machine-readable plans cover task, initiative, resource, queue, core, schema, merge and runtime operations without requiring the global Bureau repository lease.
Normal work and merge attempts using repo:/home/alex/repos/bureau fail closed; only explicitly bounded emergency recovery may use it.
Doctor warns for planned legacy tasks and blocks ready or now tasks that still request the broad Bureau repository lease.
Different task and initiative files receive different path resource keys while merge serialization uses a short dedicated gate.
The contract grants no automatic verification, queue mutation, claim, dispatch, merge or deployment authority.
Grabowski deploys the v2 consumer that rejects broad Bureau normal-work leases and proves parallel exact scopes plus short effect gates.

metadata.source: User decision that Bureau must remain always available for ecosystem registration and status updates
metadata.closeout_boundary: T011 remains planned after the Bureau-side merge and may be verified only after the Grabowski T036 runtime proof is merged and deployed.
